verdict.answer_first: A states the core answer immediately: "JWT authentication works by giving the client a signed, self-contained token after login"; B first spends a heading on "What a JWT actually is".
verdict.decisiveness: B gives clearer go/no-go guidance: "Single app, one backend? A plain server-side session... probably what you want" and "Distributed systems... JWTs earn their keep".
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on now because it includes concrete mitigations like "short-lived access tokens (5–15 min) + a revocable refresh token" and validating "exp, iss, aud".
